title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at Chevalier Theatre in Medford with a setlist focused on protest songs.
The set included tracks from 'World's Gone Wrong' and 'Good Souls Better Angels', emphasizing anti-Trump themes.
Williams concluded the concert with covers of Skip James and Neil Young, highlighting her roots influence.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Red Sox lost 8-6 to the Minnesota Twins at Fenway Park on Friday night.
Justin Slaten struggled, allowing four hits, four runs, and two home runs with one strikeout in less than an inning.
Despite nine strikeouts from starter Payton Tolle, the Red Sox bullpen faltered and could not secure the victory.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens returned to Fenway Park to throw the first pitch before a Red Sox vs. Twins game.
Clemens's son, Kody Clemens, a Twins player, caught the pitch from his father at Fenway Park.
Fans received Roger Clemens bobbleheads to commemorate his 20-strikeout game as he signed autographs outside Fenway.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White of the Boston Celtics was named to the NBA All-Defensive First Team for the 2025-26 season.
White recorded 98 blocks and 88 steals, making him the fourth Celtic to achieve such numbers in franchise history.
The Celtics finished second in the Eastern Conference with a 56-26 record, despite a first-round playoff exit.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Tatiana Maslany stars in 'Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed,' now streaming on Apple TV, about a suburban mother's descent into crime.
Oscar-winning Civil War drama 'Glory' featuring Denzel Washington is available on Netflix until June 1.
French animated feature 'Arco,' featuring an English dub cast including Mark Ruffalo, is streaming on Hulu.

title: Chad Tracy provides injury updates for Garrett Crochet, Roman Anthony
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Garrett Crochet is set for a throwing session this weekend and live batting practice next week, progressing in his rehab.
Roman Anthony is still dealing with pain tolerance issues, with no clear timeline for his return from the IL.
Trevor Story underwent sports hernia surgery, sidelining him for 6-10 weeks, impacting the Red Sox's current lineup.

title: Sam Kennedy says Red Sox looking to add bat ‘earlier than ever’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox President Sam Kennedy and Chief Baseball Officer Craig Breslow are discussing adding a hitter earlier than usual.
Boston's offensive struggles are highlighted by their 181 runs and 36 home runs, both near the bottom of MLB.
Injuries and departures have impacted the lineup, prompting the team to explore trade options ahead of the deadline.

title: Mock draft predicts Red Sox will select Bishop Feehan star in first round
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Red Sox projected by MLB.com's Jonathan Mayo to pick Brody Bumila at No. 20 in the draft.
Bumila, a 6-foot-9 left-handed pitcher, impresses with fastball-heavy style but lacks average secondary pitch.
Bishop Feehan pitcher drew significant scout attention, with mixed opinions on his draft round potential.

title: Red Sox’ Trevor Story undergoes sports hernia surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox shortstop Trevor Story had hernia surgery Thursday and will miss significant playing time.
Story's recovery is estimated to take 6-10 weeks; the team hasn't provided an official timetable.
In Story's absence, Marcelo Mayer is expected to start at shortstop for the first time in the majors.

title: Peter Wolf left one Boston art school to form the J. Geils Band. Six decades later, he earned an honorary doctorate from another.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Peter Wolf, former J. Geils Band frontman, receives honorary doctorate from Massachusetts College of Art and Design.
Wolf previously attended Boston Museum School of Fine Arts before forming the J. Geils Band decades ago.
Wolf's memoir details encounters with icons like Bob Dylan and Mick Jagger, and hints at new musical and literary projects.
